nl_proof: Explained:
Confidence 90%.
Sentences used:
(1) Squirrels can fly.
(2) Which can fly?
Proof steps:
(1) A squirrel is a squirrel  [sentence 1]
(2) If some X is a squirrel then X performs the flying event sk4 of X  [sentence 1]
(3) A squirrel performs the flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If X is a fly event and some W performs X and X is an activity and X is a capability then W is the answer  [sentence 2]
(5) technical answer unwrap step  [from question]
(6) If X is a fly event and some W performs X and X is an activity and X is a capability then W is an answer  [from steps 4, 5]
(7) If sk4 of $some_squirrel is a fly event and sk4 of $some_squirrel is an activity and sk4 of $some_squirrel is a capability then a squirrel is an answer  [from steps 3, 6]
(8) If some X is a squirrel then sk4 of X is a fly event  [sentence 1]
(9) sk4 of $some_squirrel is a fly event  [from steps 1, 8]
(10) If some X is a squirrel then the flying event sk4 of X is an activity  [sentence 1]
(11) The flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel is an activity  [from steps 1, 10]
(12) If the flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel is a capability then a squirrel is an answer  [from steps 7, 9, 11]
(13) If some X is a squirrel then normally the flying event sk4 of X is a capability  [sentence 1, confidence 90%]
(14) If a squirrel is a squirrel then a squirrel is an answer, except when the flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel is not a capability  [from steps 12, 13, confidence 90%]
(15) A squirrel is an answer, except when the flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel is not a capability  [from steps 14, 1, confidence 90%]

the flying event sk4 of $some_squirrel is not a capability
